findings.title: SCIM bearer auth bypass when token secret is unset
findings.impact: SCIM routes can be authenticated with an empty bearer value when SCIM is enabled and the configured token secret is unset.
findings.recommendation: Fail startup on missing SCIM secrets, reject empty bearer values, and compare secrets with timing-safe equality.

findings.title: Marketplace plugin install executes attacker-controlled code with host privileges
findings.impact: A plugin installed from an attacker-controlled repository can execute with host require, process, filesystem, environment, and network access.
findings.recommendation: Disable arbitrary repo plugin installs in production, require signed packages, run plugins out of process, and remove host primitives from plugin sandboxes.

findings.title: Invitation login path can skip password verification
findings.impact: An invitation-style redirect path can send login through a branch that fetches invited users without verifying their password.
findings.recommendation: Always verify passwords for credential login or require a signed, expiring invite-session proof bound to the invitation token.

findings.title: Cross-tenant data-source actions via unscoped dataSourceId lookup
findings.impact: A user with broad datasource permissions in one workspace can potentially list or create queries against a foreign workspace datasource when they know its UUID.
findings.recommendation: Scope datasource lookups by organization and reject requests when the datasource organization differs from the authenticated workspace.

findings.title: Public-app authorization and workspace stamping trust global slugs
findings.impact: Public app slug lookup can stamp a workspace header and skip feature authorization unless each feature opts out.
findings.recommendation: Use explicit public-route allowlists, avoid mutating workspace headers from slugs, and perform resource-specific organization checks.

findings.title: OAuth lacks state binding and Git SSO host is workspace-configurable
findings.impact: OAuth callbacks lack state validation and Git SSO can trust a workspace-configured host for token and userinfo responses.
findings.recommendation: Validate OAuth state and PKCE, restrict custom Git SSO hosts to allowlists, and gate or document trusted-admin SSO configuration.

findings.title: Unsafe connector query construction enables SQL and NoSQL injection
findings.impact: Connector GUI modes and template substitution can concatenate untrusted input into SQL, BigQuery, MongoDB, and SAP HANA query sinks.
findings.recommendation: Use driver-native parameters, quote identifiers safely, make raw interpolation explicit, and reject unsafe GUI-mode string substitution.

findings.title: Query-time host and connection target rewriting can redirect stored credentials
findings.impact: Query options can alter OpenAPI URLs or database connection targets while using stored credentials.
findings.recommendation: Encode URL path segments, remove or permission-gate host overrides, build URLs structurally, and validate targets against allowlists.

findings.title: Outbound connectors allow SSRF to internal resources
findings.impact: Server-side connectors can request loopback, private network, link-local, or metadata endpoints without centralized egress controls.
findings.recommendation: Centralize outbound HTTP, restrict schemes, block private and link-local IP ranges after DNS resolution, and add connector allowlists.

findings.title: REST connector cookie bridging allows session fixation
findings.impact: An attacker-controlled REST upstream can cause ToolJet to set reserved cookies such as tj_auth_token on the ToolJet origin.
findings.recommendation: Block ToolJet-reserved cookie names in both directions and only allow cookie forwarding for explicitly trusted same-site upstreams.

findings.title: Stored comment mention rendering XSS
findings.impact: Stored comment content can be transformed into unsanitized HTML and rendered in workspace users' browsers.
findings.recommendation: Render mentions as React nodes or sanitize after mention highlighting immediately before insertion.

findings.title: Table and highlighted-cell rendering can inject unsanitized HTML
findings.impact: Datasource values or editable table values can reach dangerouslySetInnerHTML in table rendering paths.
findings.recommendation: Prefer text rendering for string cells and sanitize every value after all highlighting and formatting transformations.

findings.title: Credentialed CORS reflection and embed cookies enable cross-origin authenticated access
findings.impact: Any hostile origin can issue credentialed requests and potentially read responses when reflected CORS and SameSite=None cookies are enabled.
findings.recommendation: Use explicit CORS allowlists and enforce CSRF tokens or strict origin checks for cookie-authenticated requests.

findings.title: File UUID lookup lacks tenant or ownership scope
findings.impact: Any authenticated user can fetch a file by known UUID without organization or ownership scoping.
findings.recommendation: Add ownership and organization metadata to files or authorize through the referencing resource before serving bytes.

findings.title: gRPC proto directory validation warns but still reads outside allowed roots
findings.impact: A builder-controlled gRPC datasource can cause the server to scan and read proto files from unexpected filesystem paths.
findings.recommendation: Reject absolute or outside-root proto paths and enforce an allowlisted proto directory root.

findings.title: Editors can publish apps and change public slugs
findings.impact: Users with app edit permission can expose apps anonymously and claim globally unique slugs.
findings.recommendation: Separate app publishing and slug changes into admin-controlled permissions with explicit audit events.

findings.title: Frontend sanitizer, PDF, and editor dependencies remain vulnerable in rendered surfaces
findings.impact: Vulnerable DOMPurify, jsPDF, jspdf-autotable, Draft.js, and immutable versions sit on rendered HTML, rich-text, and PDF export paths.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade the affected packages, replace or isolate Draft.js, and add rendering and PDF export regression tests.

findings.title: Server and plugin outbound dependencies expose SSRF, XML, and command-injection risk
findings.impact: Plugin axios, AWS SDK XML builder chains, and the pinned systeminformation override affect outbound connector and host metrics paths.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade axios and AWS SDK chains, remove or update the systeminformation override, and smoke test connectors and OTel metrics.

findings.title: Reset and invitation tokens are plaintext, unexpired, and weakly throttled
findings.impact: A database snapshot can expose reset and invite tokens that remain usable without expiry enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Hash tokens at rest, add expiry and rate limits, and revalidate inviter authority when invites are accepted.

findings.title: Workflow webhook tokens are plaintext and replayable
findings.impact: Captured workflow trigger requests can be replayed and database readers can obtain working webhook tokens.
findings.recommendation: Hash webhook tokens, compare with timing-safe equality, support rotation, and add HMAC timestamp and nonce replay protection.

findings.title: Folder update is not scoped by organization
findings.impact: A user with update permission and a foreign folder UUID can rename a folder in another organization.
findings.recommendation: Add organizationId to the update predicate and cover update and delete scoping with regression tests.

findings.title: Data-source update audit metadata can retain plaintext credentials
findings.impact: Plaintext datasource credentials can be preserved in audit metadata before encrypted storage is applied.
findings.recommendation: Redact sensitive option keys before setting audit metadata and test datasource credential updates.

findings.title: Telemetry and HTTP error logging can expose PII or upstream secrets
findings.impact: Request bodies, PII, upstream error bodies, or full HTTP error objects can reach logs or third-party telemetry.
findings.recommendation: Disable default PII collection, redact bodies and upstream errors, and log structured metadata only.

findings.title: Reachable upload, email, and route parsing dependencies allow moderate DoS
findings.impact: Vulnerable multer, nodemailer, path-to-regexp, and Nest core versions are on reachable upload, email, and route parsing paths.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Nest and multer, review a nodemailer major upgrade, and add endpoint-level throttling.

findings.title: Global request body and parameter limits are overly large
findings.impact: Large global JSON and URL-encoded parameter limits can be used for moderate resource exhaustion.
findings.recommendation: Lower global limits, add endpoint-specific limits, and throttle public auth, import, and upload routes.

findings.title: Constants and secrets regex can overmatch server-side substitutions
findings.impact: Incorrect regex precedence can resolve a broader set of constants, secrets, or server globals than intended.
findings.recommendation: Use a grouped matcher for constants, secrets, and globals.server references and add negative-match tests.

findings.title: Deployment defaults and missing secret validation can expose self-hosted instances
findings.impact: Known-default or missing secrets can expose encrypted credentials, PostgREST signing, or queue dashboard payloads in self-hosted deployments.
findings.recommendation: Fail startup on missing or known-default secrets, generate install-time secrets, and disable privileged dashboards without strong auth.

findings.title: Auth cookie Secure flag depends on TOOLJET_HOST string parsing
findings.impact: Proxy deployments can accidentally issue non-secure auth cookies when TOOLJET_HOST uses an internal HTTP URL.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit cookie security configuration and trusted proxy handling instead of relying on host string parsing.

findings.title: Permissive CSP weakens XSS containment
findings.impact: Inline script, eval, wildcard connection, and broad frame allowances make confirmed or likely XSS issues easier to exploit.
findings.recommendation: Remove unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval, add nonces or hashes, narrow connect-src, and set explicit frame-ancestors.

findings.title: Build-time supply-chain and EOL dependency hygiene risk remains
findings.impact: Deprecated and build-time dependency chains increase maintenance and supply-chain exposure for self-hosted builds.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade bcrypt, replace abandoned libraries, remove unused dependencies, and fail CI on new critical or high runtime advisories.
